description_long: This is a weathered bust sculpture of a bearded man, mounted on a rectangular stone base. The figure is depicted from the chest up, with detailed facial features and textured hair, suggesting an expressive and classical style. The base of the sculpture features an inscription, with the name 'ARISTOTLE' clearly visible on the front, indicating the subject is the ancient Greek philosopher Aristotle. The surface of the bust is rough and uneven, with visible signs of aging and erosion, giving it a historic and venerable appearance. The back of the sculpture is less detailed, with a more roughly hewn finish, while the sides show the pronounced profile and shoulders of the figure. The entire piece sits atop a solid plinth, which appears to be made of stone or concrete.
description: A weathered bust of Aristotle with a rough, textured surface, mounted on a stone base with an inscribed nameplate. The sculpture features detailed facial features and a classical style.
description_view_0: Front view showing Aristotle's face, chest, and the inscribed nameplate on the base.
description_view_1: Left profile view highlighting the textured hair, nose, and the depth of the bust's shoulders.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rough, unfinished back of the head and shoulders, with minimal detail.
description_view_3: Right profile view showing the side of the face, hair, and the base's edge, with some visible surface damage.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture appears isolated, possibly for cataloging or display purposes.
